markdown:
#  Can we import our third-party timecard entries to work with the 'Timesheet Hours' column in Procore's Budget tool? 
##  Background 
If your company collects timecard entries using third-party software instead of Procore's Resource Tracking tools, you can still import your timecard data into Procore's 'Timesheet Hours' column to track labor in your budget.
##  Answer 
Yes. If your company does NOT use Procore's Resource Tracking tools, you can develop your own custom or third-party custom integration for inputting your timecard entries into Procore. Your custom integration can input your entries to Procore's Timecards tool using the API, which enables you to view timecard entries in the 'Timesheet Hours' column of a budget view. 
Similar to how a user configures the Company level Timesheets tool's 'Default Cost Type for Timecards' setting to 'Labor' (see [Configure Advanced Settings: Company Level Timesheets](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/timesheets-company/tutorials/configure-advanced-settings-company-level-timesheets)), this API solution sets your third-party time entries to 'Labor' (or any other cost type that you would prefer to use) and stores your data in the [Timecard](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/timecard-company/) tool.
Here are the steps for creating or updating a timecard entry using the API.
  1. Use the [List Line Item (Cost Code) Types](https://developers.procore.com/reference/line-item-types-cost-types#list-line-item-types) endpoint to determine the cost types associated with your account.
  2. In the list of cost types returned, locate the cost type with a `base_type `value of 'labor' (or any other cost type that you would prefer to use) and note its `id` value. Because each Procore company account can create a custom list of cost types, cost type identifiers are not consistent across accounts. You will use this `id `value in the next step.
  3. To create a new timecard entry or update an existing one, use the [Create Timecard Entry](https://developers.procore.com/reference/timecard-entries#create-timecard-entry) or Update Timecard Entry endpoints respectively. In the body of your request, use the (cost type) `id` value you retrieved from the previous step as the value for the `line_item_type_id` attribute.


#####  Important
  * The 'Real-Time Labor Productivity' and 'Labor Productivity Cost' budget views are supported and recommended for use by companies using Procore's Resource Tracking tools. However, using the Procore API, you can use the API to import your time entries and add the 'Timesheet Hours' column to a budget view. 
  * To learn more about how the 'Timesheet Hours' column works with Procore's Resource Tracking tools, see:
    * [How do I add the 'Timesheet Hours' column to a budget view?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-how-do-i-add-the-timesheet-hours-column-to-a-budget-view)
    * [Resource Tracking and Project Financials: Setup Guide](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/resource-tracking-and-project-financials-setup-guide/)
    * [Set Up the Procore Labor Productivity Cost Budget View](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/admin-company/tutorials/set-up-the-procore-labor-productivity-cost-budget-view)
  * If you add the 'Timesheet Hours' column to a budget view when you are not using Procore's Resource Tracking tools or the API solution documented in this FAQ, the value in the 'Timesheet Hours' column on your budget view will always be reflected as zero.


## See Also
  * [What are Procore's standard budget views?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-are-procores-standard-budget-views)
  * [How do I add the 'Timesheet Hours' column to a budget view?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-how-do-i-add-the-timesheet-hours-column-to-a-budget-view)
  * [How does the 'Actuals By Date' filter work on a budget view?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-how-does-the-actual-date-filter-work-on-a-budget-view)
title: Can we import our third-party timecard entries to work with the 'Timesheet Hours' column in Procore's Budget tool?
description: How to use the Procore API to populate the 'Timesheet Hours' column when your company's Procore Account is not using Procore's Resource Tracking tools.
